name: Mouth of the Guadalhorce River - circular from Princesa-Huelin metro via Paseo Marítimo
name_original: Desembocadura Río Guadalhorce – circular desde metro Princesa-Huelin por Paseo Marítimo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Málaga offers diverse and rugged landscapes, making it an exceptional destination for outdoor activities. Nestled between mountains and the Mediterranean Sea, the region features extensive natural parks like Montes de Málaga and Sierra de Mijas. Riders encounter varied terrain, from pine forests and rolling hills to unpaved mountain roads and trails with significant elevation changes, providing a range of experiences for all skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Málaga The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural Montes de Málaga – circular al Mirador del Cochino desde la Explanada de Contadoras, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes through the pine forests of Montes de Málaga Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Capitana Loop from Rincón de la Victoria, a moderate 17.2 miles (27.7 km) path. This route provides varied surfaces and views, often including coastal sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Mouth of the Guadalhorce River - circular from Princesa-Huelin metro via Paseo Marítimo, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas and river plains, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Málaga is defined by pine forests, rolling hills, and varied surfaces from mountain ranges to coastal paths. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 6000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Málaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I expect in Málaga?
A: Málaga offers a diverse range of mountain biking terrain, from extensive pine forests and rolling hills in areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Málaga Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; to unpaved mountain roads and varied surfaces in the Sierra de Mijas, which can include fixed rock, switchbacks, sand, and loose rocks. You'll find everything from smooth singletracks to challenging, rocky descents, with many routes being natural forest singletracks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for all skill levels in Málaga?
A: Yes, Málaga caters to all skill levels. The region offers a wide array of trails, from easy coastal rides and river plains paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994604693/mouth-of-the-guadalhorce-river-circular-from-princesa-huelin-metro-via-paseo-maritimo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Guadalhorce River - circular from Princesa-Huelin metro via Paseo Marítimo&lt;/a&gt;, to challenging mountain ascents with significant elevation changes, like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987196182/parque-natural-montes-de-malaga-circular-al-mirador-del-cochino-desde-la-explanada-de-contadoras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Montes de Málaga – circular al Mirador del Cochino desde la Explanada de Contadoras&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Málaga?
A: Many trails offer stunning natural beauty and landmarks. You can ride through the pine forests of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Málaga Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy coastal views, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3166517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalhorce River Mouth&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead to viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/132339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the city and coast.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Málaga?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Málaga are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987196182/parque-natural-montes-de-malaga-circular-al-mirador-del-cochino-desde-la-explanada-de-contadoras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Montes de Málaga – circular al Mirador del Cochino&lt;/a&gt; and the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994604693/mouth-of-the-guadalhorce-river-circular-from-princesa-huelin-metro-via-paseo-maritimo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Guadalhorce River - circular from Princesa-Huelin metro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Málaga?
A: Málaga's favorable climate, with mountains near the sea, makes it suitable for outdoor activities year-round. The dry, dusty terrain of areas like Sierra de Mijas is particularly popular for professional riders during winter, but generally, you can enjoy mountain biking in Málaga throughout most seasons.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Málaga?
A: Yes, there are options suitable for families. Trails along coastal areas and river plains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994604693/mouth-of-the-guadalhorce-river-circular-from-princesa-huelin-metro-via-paseo-maritimo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Guadalhorce River - circular from Princesa-Huelin metro&lt;/a&gt;, tend to be easier and flatter, making them more accessible for family outings. Always check the difficulty grade and elevation profile before heading out.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails near Málaga?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994604693/mouth-of-the-guadalhorce-river-circular-from-princesa-huelin-metro-via-paseo-maritimo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Guadalhorce River circular&lt;/a&gt; at about 10.4 miles (16.8 km), or longer, more challenging rides such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1027237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Málaga – Boar Viewpoint loop from Ciudad de la Justicia&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 32.6 miles (52.5 km).

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Málaga?
A: The mountain bike routes in Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 700 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic coastal paths, and the extensive network of natural forest singletracks that offer flowy rides and diverse experiences.

Q: Can I reach mountain bike trailheads in Málaga using public transport?
A: Some trails are accessible via public transport. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994604693/mouth-of-the-guadalhorce-river-circular-from-princesa-huelin-metro-via-paseo-maritimo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Guadalhorce River circular&lt;/a&gt; starts near the Princesa-Huelin metro station, making it convenient for those relying on public transport. For routes further afield in natural parks, a car might be more practical.

Q: Are there any advanced mountain biking trails in Málaga?
A: Yes, Málaga offers several advanced and difficult trails for experienced riders. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987196182/parque-natural-montes-de-malaga-circular-al-mirador-del-cochino-desde-la-explanada-de-contadoras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Montes de Málaga – circular al Mirador del Cochino&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes and challenging terrain. The Sierra de Mijas also has trails known for varied surfaces and challenging descents, popular with professional riders.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer views of Málaga city?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the surrounding mountains provide spectacular views of Málaga city and the coastline. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1027237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Málaga – Boar Viewpoint loop from Ciudad de la Justicia&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer panoramic vistas, including the city below.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on mountain bike trails in Málaga?
A: While specific wildlife sightings are never guaranteed, areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Málaga Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park are known for their biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, small mammals, and local flora, especially in the quieter, more remote sections of the trails.
tips: There are numerous trails around the mouth of the Guadalhorce. On the trails closest to the lagoons, cycling has been prohibited since 2020 due to its degree of protection, but there are many alternatives going parallel to the river.
Oasis of peace in complete nature, kingdom of waterfowl
At this point you can find out about the different paths along the river and those sections with restrictions for bicycles (those that pass next to the lagoons).

waypoints.name: Trails of the Guadalhorce River Mouth
